keywords: functional mapping of cerebellothalamic networks after focused ultrasound
topics.title: Focused Ultrasound and Connectivity in Essential Tremor
topics.description: Studies of essential tremor focus on focused ultrasound thalamotomy and related MR-guided procedures, with emphasis on Vim targeting and motor improvement. Work also examines cerebellothalamic network mapping, connectivity fingerprints, and longitudinal structural-functional changes linked to durable outcomes.

topics.title: Long COVID Brain and Vascular Dysfunction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neurological and vascular alterations associated with long COVID across age groups, including cognitive fog, cerebral hypoperfusion, and impaired vascular reactivity. It also includes allostatic, interoceptive, and network-level changes such as limbic DMN hyperactivity and neural dissociation.

topics.title: Brain Injury Networks and PTSD Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on structural and network changes associated with traumatic brain injury and related conditions such as concussion and PTSD. It emphasizes altered cortical and limbic organization, multimodal brain network analysis, and links to recovery and adverse outcomes.

topics.title: Locus Coeruleus Imaging and Network Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on MRI-based investigation of the human locus coeruleus, including contrast, integrity, and coupling measures across multimodal imaging approaches. The studies link locus coeruleus features to salience network organization, cortical gradients, and behavioral or clinical factors such as impulsivity and trauma history.

keywords: spatiotemporal neural correlates of chronic widespread pain
topics.title: Neural Signatures of Chronic Pain
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain and spinal cord correlates of chronic and widespread pain, including pain perception, sensitivity, and network-level signatures. It also includes related factors such as central sensitization, catastrophizing, and functional connectivity pathways underlying pain processing.

keywords: rich club eigenvector centrality predicts differential treatment response in chronic low back pain
topics.title: Pain Mechanisms and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on pain conditions, analgesic response, and placebo-related effects across chronic and clinical pain syndromes. It also includes neuroimaging and brain network findings linked to treatment response and pain mechanisms.

keywords: brain sensitivity across the menstrual cycle an eeg study
menopause specific hippocampal subfield volumes and memory at midlife a period of change
topics.title: Menstrual and Menopausal Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how menstrual and menopausal transitions relate to brain activity, connectivity, and structure in women. It emphasizes hippocampal, memory, and emotion-regulation changes across midlife and hormone-related states.

keywords: functional network remapping following brain tumor surgery
topics.title: Glioma-Driven Network Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how glioma and glioblastoma alter brain networks, connectivity, and structural organization, especially around tumor location and surgery. It also includes prediction of functional recovery, deficits, and survival outcomes using network-level changes.

keywords: brain metabolic networks underlying integration and disease
gradient organization of cortical metabolic connectivity
multi site multi modal mrs study
topics.title: Brain Metabolic Connectivity in Health and Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain metabolic networks and their organization using MRSI and connectomics. It emphasizes metabolic gradients, network signatures, and regional integration across development, aging, and disease.

topics.title: Psilocybin and Large-Scale Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster centers on how psilocybin and other psychedelic states alter large-scale brain organization, functional connectivity, and dynamic propagation patterns. It also includes analyses using EEG microstates, neural avalanches, and related measures of complexity and modularity.

keywords: long term aerobic exercise reconfigures directed resting state eeg network topology
age related reorganization and plasticity of functional gradients in primary motor cortex
topics.title: Exercise-Induced Brain Network Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how physical exercise and related interventions alter brain network organization, functional connectivity, and cortical gradients across age groups. It links these neural changes to cognitive gain and motor outcomes, including plasticity in motor and attention networks.

topics.title: Mindfulness and Meditative Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on mindfulness and meditation interventions and their associations with changes in functional brain networks, coupling, and brain structure/function. It also includes reported cognitive and sleep-related outcomes such as cognitive gains and insomnia improvement.

topics.title: Neural Signatures of Tinnitus
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural correlates and brain alterations associated with tinnitus, including chronic and somatic subtypes, loudness, and suppression. It also highlights structural and neurochemical findings across auditory and prefrontal regions.

topics.title: Epilepsy localization and connectivity studies
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy subtypes, especially focal and drug-resistant cases, and the identification of epileptogenic zones in pediatric and clinical populations. It also highlights EEG/SEEG, MRI, MEG, and connectivity analyses used to characterize epileptic brain activity.

topics.title: Glycemic Effects on Brain Structure
topics.description: These studies examine how type 2 diabetes, prediabetes, and acute hyperglycemia relate to brain morphometry, gray matter loss, and cognitive impairment. Several abstracts also involve longitudinal and multimodal MRI approaches, including glymphatic-related measures and glucose manipulation paradigms.

keywords: cognitive task evoke brain body interactions
topics.title: Neurometabolic Coupling During Brain Activity
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional brain responses relate to metabolic signals, especially using combined PET-fMRI and related imaging approaches. It highlights coupling, uncoupling, and task-related activation/deactivation patterns across cognitive and emotional states.

topics.title: Deep Brain Stimulation Circuit Mapping and Outcomes
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep brain stimulation for movement and neuropsychiatric disorders, especially Parkinson disease, with emphasis on subthalamic targets and outcome prediction. It highlights circuit-based analyses, imaging-informed brain models, and programming approaches to personalize stimulation response.

topics.title: Prodromal Parkinsonian Neurodegeneration and Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinsonian and related neurodegenerative disorders, especially prodromal stages, phenotype conversion, and linked symptoms such as cognitive decline and autonomic failure. It also highlights pathological and structural biomarkers including substantia nigra involvement, alpha synuclein pathology, and iron accumulation.

topics.title: Stress-Related Brain Signatures in Mental Health
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how stress and related clinical conditions are linked to brain signatures, connectivity, and interoceptive processing. It includes work on biomarkers, functional integration, and cerebral mechanisms across mental health and disorders such as depression and fibromyalgia.

keywords: python toolbox for deep neural networks
topics.title: Neurofeedback and Neuroimaging Tool Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on neurofeedback and neuroimaging research, with emphasis on real-time implementation, auditory processing, and analysis of physiological signals. It also includes open-source Python toolboxes and platforms for processing, quality assessment, and deep learning support.

keywords: collaborative infrastructure around shared tool interfaces for neuroimaging
topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Infrastructure and Workflow Tools
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, collaborative neuroimaging infrastructure built around BIDS-based data organization, containerized workflows, and shared tool interfaces. It also includes platform development for visualization, cloud execution, and clinically actionable analysis pipelines.

keywords: resting state fmri cerebrospinal fluid oscillations
topics.title: CSF-Glymphatic Coupling and Brain Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster focuses on interactions between cerebrospinal fluid oscillations, glymphatic function, and global brain activity across rest, sleep, and lifespan contexts. It also includes normative and network-level analyses of CSF-related dynamics and coupling with BOLD signals.

keywords: multimodal fmri study of trigger processing
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Misophonia Triggers
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural basis of misophonia, especially how trigger processing and trigger response relate to interoception, heartbeat signals, and impaired insular prefrontal connectivity. Studies use multimodal fMRI, EEG, connectome analysis, and dynamic causal analysis to probe neural mechanisms underlying aversive reactions to triggering stimuli.

keywords: human brain dynamics change across the lifespan
topics.title: Cognitive and Brain Aging Across the Lifespan
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging affects executive function, memory, motor learning, and broader brain dynamics across the adult lifespan. It also highlights compensatory changes and functional dedifferentiation as signatures of healthy and declining cognitive aging.

keywords: dimensions of psychiatry for clinical prediction
topics.title: MRI-Based Psychiatric Classification and Prediction
topics.description: This cluster focuses on machine learning approaches for psychiatric classification and clinical prediction using structural MRI and related neuroimaging features. It spans bipolar disorder, schizophrenia spectrum disorder, and unipolar depression, with emphasis on patient stratification and multi-site detection performance.

keywords: gamlss method for human neuroimaging case studies
toward derive a normative atlas of resting state human magnetoencephalography
topics.title: Normative Neuroimaging Models and Atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on normative modeling approaches in human neuroimaging, including calibration, transferable models, and atlas construction. The abstracts also span applications to diffusion MRI, resting-state magnetoencephalography, and disease-related neuroimaging phenotypes.

topics.title: Neural Network Alterations in Hallucinations
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neural mechanisms and network-level dysfunction underlying visual and auditory hallucinations across psychiatric and neurodegenerative conditions. It emphasizes connectivity alterations, brain dynamics, and biomarkers related to hallucination severity and awareness.

topics.title: Cerebellar Development and Lifespan Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on cerebellar development, morphology, and functional connectivity across the lifespan, with attention to neonatal and preterm-related alterations. It also includes cross-species and high-resolution mapping efforts that support cerebellar atlas construction and neuroanatomical characterization.

keywords: bimanual vr mirror therapy modulates sensorimotor network activation after stroke
topics.title: Motor Representation and Action Observation After Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on how action observation, imagery, and movement execution relate to motor representations and sensorimotor network reorganization, especially in stroke. It also includes work on grasping, finger representations, and cortical-cerebellar control within rehabilitation and motor recovery contexts.

keywords: convolutional neural network base spinal cord volume quantification
mri base spinal cord morphology link
topics.title: Cervical Myelopathy and Cortical Reorganization
topics.description: This cluster centers on degenerative cervical myelopathy and related changes in the human cervical spinal cord, including decompression effects and spinal cord volume quantification. It also includes brain and cortical reorganization measures, cytoarchitectonic mapping, and neural network-based morphology analysis.

topics.title: Neural Processing of Smell and Odor Perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how smell and body odor are processed in the brain, including odor-induced brain activation, self body odor comparison, and neural mechanisms underlying olfactory identification. It also includes studies of olfactory dysfunction, congenital anosmia, and related patient and female responses to odor and fear.

keywords: the effect of iron deposition on cognitive fatigue
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Cognitive Fatigue
topics.description: This cluster centers on fatigue and cognitive fatigue, especially in multiple sclerosis, and investigates neural correlates such as iron deposition, glutamatergic change, and monoaminergic circuit involvement. It also includes network-based and multimodal approaches to localize fatigue-related effects and relate them to neuropsychology and mental effort.

topics.title: Lesion Network Mapping in Neuropsychiatry
topics.description: This cluster centers on lesion-based network approaches linking focal brain lesions and distributed circuits to psychiatric, behavioral, and self-related symptoms. It includes network mapping, meta-analytic lesion studies, and symptom-related circuit inference across disorders and behaviors.

topics.title: Ketamine Effects on Brain Connectivity and Response
topics.description: This cluster focuses on ketamine-related changes in brain connectivity, neural information dynamics, and perceptual response across healthy adults and major depression. It also highlights dose-dependent pharmacodynamics and markers of therapeutic response.

topics.title: White Matter Hyperintensities in Aging and Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter hyperintensities and related white matter changes across aging, small vessel disease, and neurodegenerative conditions such as ALS and Alzheimer disease spectrum. It links WMH burden and regional patterns to cognitive effects, cerebral hemodynamics, and microstructural insights in older and geriatric cohorts.

keywords: executive control salience network structure function coupling
microstates pipeline for source level connectivity and classification in multiple sclerosis
topics.title: Multiple Sclerosis Brain Network and Motor Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on multiple sclerosis and how multimodal MRI, EEG microstates, and source-level connectivity are used to study brain network function. It also highlights motor-cognitive coupling, processing speed, and dexterity-related markers of cognitive and functional impairment.

topics.title: Quantitative MRI of Brain Iron and Myelin
topics.description: This cluster centers on quantitative MRI approaches for characterizing brain iron and myelin across cortical layers and regions. It includes studies of age-related and disease-related microstructural changes, especially in healthy cortex and cognitive decline contexts.

topics.title: MRI and Biomarkers in Multiple Sclerosis
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and microstructural MRI markers of multiple sclerosis, including lesion burden, brain volume loss, and thalamic atrophy, with links to disability and neurodegeneration. It also includes predictive and normative modeling approaches for diagnosis and prognosis, alongside segmentation and deep learning methods.

topics.title: Stroke Lesions, Connectivity, and Outcome Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke lesion characteristics and brain network changes relate to cognitive, language, and motor outcomes. It also includes predictive approaches for acute stroke severity and longer-term recovery using lesion mapping and connectivity-based measures.

keywords: early parietofrontal connectivity predicts visuomotor recovery after stroke
topics.title: Motor Network Reorganization After Stroke
topics.description: This cluster centers on how stroke disrupts motor networks and how structural and functional connectivity relate to recovery, especially visuomotor and motor control outcomes. It also includes predictors of recovery such as parietofrontal connectivity, corticospinal tract integrity, and network reorganization.

keywords: midlife diet quality shapes late life brain health
race ethnicity impact education cognition association
topics.title: Cognitive Reserve and Brain Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cognitive reserve, lifestyle, and demographic factors relate to brain aging, cognition, and resilience in later life. It also includes structural and network-level brain markers associated with executive function, memory, and pathology.

keywords: accurate voxel wise brain age prediction
convolutional neural network for brain age prediction
topics.title: Brain Age Prediction in Clinical Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on estimating brain age from MRI using machine learning and deep learning models. The studies emphasize prediction accuracy, voxel-wise mapping, and the clinical value of brain age gaps for detecting accelerated aging and disease-related deviation.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging simultaneous integration of eeg
functional mri compatible cortical wide field fluorescent
topics.title: Multimodal Neuroimaging and Functional Signal Integration
topics.description: This cluster centers on combining optical, hemodynamic, MRI, EEG, and MEG approaches to study brain activity with higher spatial-temporal resolution. The abstracts emphasize simultaneous measurements, multimodal evidence, and novel signal types for mapping neural and neurovascular dynamics.

keywords: ml models for multiple system atrophy classification use mri
topics.title: Robust MRI Classification for Neurodegeneration
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning approaches for classifying neurodegenerative diseases from MRI, including multiple system atrophy and Alzheimer disease. A major emphasis is placed on robustness, analytic stability, atlas selection, and reliable neuroimaging-based diagnosis.

keywords: foundation models identify eeg biomarkers for alzheimer 's
clinical translation of eeg dementia predictors
atlas guided region wise shapley value
topics.title: Explainable AI for Dementia Detection
topics.description: This cluster centers on applying machine learning and explainable AI to detect Alzheimer-related conditions, predict cognitive decline, and support early diagnosis. It emphasizes EEG and imaging biomarkers, clinical translation, and uncertainty-aware interpretation of model outputs.

topics.title: Fast fMRI dynamics and variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on fast fMRI signals, temporal structure, and factors affecting measurement quality and interpretability across resting and task-related data. It also includes analyses of physiological modulation, prediction, and individual differences such as sex difference.

topics.title: Adolescent Sleep and Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on how adolescent sleep patterns and disturbances relate to brain structure, functional connectivity, and cognitive outcomes such as attention and language cognition. It also highlights longitudinal links between sleep characteristics and developmental brain measures.

keywords: dynamic co activation brain states across sleep stage reveal by
topics.title: Sleep State Dynamics and Neural Signatures
topics.description: This cluster centers on how sleep stages and transitions shape brain activity, EEG features, and dynamic connectivity patterns. It includes studies of sleep-specific neural signatures, k-complexes, spindles, and state-dependent changes across NREM and REM sleep.

topics.title: Multimodal Subtyping of Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster focuses on heterogeneity and subtype formation across the Alzheimer disease continuum, especially in preclinical and prodromal stages. Studies integrate amyloid and tau PET, MRI, and multimodal fusion to characterize subtype-specific deposition patterns and individualized brain atrophy.

keywords: functional brain alterations in preclinical alzheimer 's
connectome harmonics reveal alzheimer tau connectivity alignment predict cognitive decline
topics.title: Tau Imaging and Progression in Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on tau pathology in Alzheimer disease, especially PET-based imaging, regional tau patterns, and longitudinal tau progression. It also includes studies linking tau to cognitive decline, functional brain alterations, and resilience or reserve mechanisms.

topics.title: Craving and Connectivity in Addiction
topics.description: This cluster centers on addiction-related craving, reward, and cue reactivity across substance and behavioral addictions. It also emphasizes neuroimaging findings on frontostriatal and corticostriatal connectivity during abstinence and drug cue exposure.

keywords: striatal social prediction error tracking moderates susceptibility
topics.title: Dopamine and social prediction in learning
topics.description: This cluster centers on reinforcement learning, prediction error processing, and orbitofrontal-striatal updating in humans, with a strong emphasis on dopaminergic mechanisms in midbrain and ventral tegmental area. It also includes social influence, effort, and willingness, suggesting work on how social context and motivation shape learning and choice.

keywords: cross species evaluation of atomoxetine on default mode network connectivity
network connectivity patterns associate with metabolic syndrome and
cerebellar structural networks link dual physical cognitive impairment
topics.title: Default Mode Connectivity in Cognitive and Metabolic Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on default mode network connectivity, structure-function coupling, and network connectivity patterns in relation to cognitive impairment, preclinical alzheimer disease, and metabolic syndrome. It also includes cross-species evaluation of atomoxetine and potential markers in older adults and APOE-related contexts.

keywords: resting state co activation pattern alteration
recurrence networks of eeg reveal altered state space organization in alzheimer ’s disease
topics.title: Dynamic Network Changes in Alzheimer Disease
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease across the preclinical and cognitive decline continuum, including APOE-related risk and mild cognitive impairment heterogeneity. The abstracts emphasize altered brain network topology, dynamic functional connectivity, and EEG/fMRI markers of neurodegeneration.

topics.title: Thermoregulation and Aging in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how thermoregulatory stress and human thermoregulation relate to brain, cardiac, and cardiovascular responses across aging and healthy aging. It also includes network-level and temporal functional changes, with links to depressive symptom and age-related change.

topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Bases of Decision Making
topics.description: This cluster centers on decision making under risk and value, including food choice, reward learning, and the neural correlates of optimal risk taking. It also includes computational and behavioral factors such as choice history, impulse control, and real world risky behavior.

topics.title: Neural and Developmental Bases of Social Cognition
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain supports social cognition, including fairness, empathy, perspective taking, and responses to social norm violation. It also emphasizes developmental change across childhood and adolescence, with links to neural synchrony and network reorganization.

keywords: cortico subcortical mechanisms of dynamic music reward processing
neurometabolic mechanisms of dynamic musical reward processing
the effect of music on mental concentration level
topics.title: Dynamic Music Reward and Emotion
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how music elicits reward, pleasure, and emotional responses, especially through cortico-subcortical and neurometabolic mechanisms. It also includes related studies of physiological reactions and attention effects during music perception.

keywords: the protective role of memory suppression
topics.title: Trauma, Regulation, and Resilience
topics.description: This cluster centers on how childhood trauma and social stress relate to emotion regulation, memory suppression, and resilience across neurofunctional and transdiagnostic studies. It also highlights protective mechanisms involving prefrontal-amygdala pathways and vulnerability to emotional distress and perinatal depression.

topics.title: Neurofeedback and Brain-Computer Training
topics.description: Studies focus on neurofeedback and related brain-computer approaches for training self-regulation using EEG, fMRI, and fNIR methods. The cluster also includes analytic tools and neural measures used to decode, modulate, and assess brain states during training.

keywords: facial emotion modulates competition dependent spatial biases
topics.title: Neural Processing of Facial Emotion Biases
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the brain processes emotionally salient faces, including fear, pain, ambiguity, and negativity bias. It also includes neuroimaging and connectivity approaches used to study face-selective and ventral occipitotemporal regions.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Emotion and Valence
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how the human brain represents and regulates emotion, especially valence, arousal, and affective transitions. It emphasizes amygdala-centered activity, dynamic connectivity, and neural signatures during emotion processing and naturalistic viewing.

keywords: the neural underpinnings of social threat
topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Fear and Social Threat
topics.description: This cluster centers on how fear conditioning, extinction, and threat processing are represented in the brain, especially in relation to amygdala and prefrontal regulation. It also highlights anxiety and social threat processes, including social anxiety disorder and emotional reactivity.

keywords: emotion regulation effort predicts supplementary motor area activation
neural correlates of state and trait emotion regulation a large scale fmri study
topics.title: Neural and Behavioral Bases of Emotion Regulation
topics.description: This cluster centers on emotion regulation and cognitive reappraisal, examining how state and trait regulation relate to neural correlates, amygdala regulation, and broader brain activity. It also includes large-scale fMRI findings and contextual factors such as stress, social support, and real-world flexibility.

keywords: white matter development in youth prenatally expose
topics.title: Prenatal Alcohol Exposure and Brain Development
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how prenatal alcohol exposure relates to brain development, including white matter changes, cerebellar alterations, and broader morphology in children and youth. Studies also examine cognitive and spatial navigation outcomes, often using cohort or MRI-based approaches.

topics.title: OCD Networks and Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster centers on obsessive compulsive disorder in youth, with emphasis on frontostriatal and broader brain-network alterations linked to compulsivity and comorbid anxiety. It also includes treatment-related work on CBT and neural predictors of response, along with inhibitory control and semantic inhibition mechanisms.

topics.title: Brain Development in Preterm Children and Adolescents
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental brain differences and cognitive outcomes across childhood and adolescence, with emphasis on white matter, microstructure, and connectome changes. It frequently examines how preterm birth relates to normative brain development and later neuroanatomical or cognitive trajectories.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity and Metabolic Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on brain structure and functional connectivity changes associated with obesity and anorexia nervosa, including biomarker candidates and related metabolic factors such as leptin. It also highlights developmental and sex differences, with studies spanning adolescents, white matter, and executive functioning.

topics.title: Aperiodic EEG Dynamics and Cognitive State
topics.description: This cluster centers on aperiodic and alpha-band EEG features, including spectral complexity, peak frequency, and broadband activity. The studies link these neural dynamics to cognition, cognitive state change, and brain network connectivity across age and related conditions.

keywords: maternal distress × cortico limbic connectivity predicts
prenatal maternal mood modulates longitudinal trajectories of brain development
topics.title: Maternal Mood and Infant Brain Development
topics.description: Studies of how prenatal and postpartum maternal mental health, exposures, and distress relate to fetal and neonatal brain development. The cluster highlights longitudinal brain connectivity and structural differences linked to later neurodevelopmental and psychiatric risk.

keywords: tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation
tms coil orientation modulates motor threshold estimation and cortical silent period duration
topics.title: Personalized TMS Mapping and Excitability
topics.description: This cluster centers on transcranial magnetic stimulation research aimed at motor mapping, threshold estimation, and cortical excitability measurement. It also includes optimization and modeling approaches for personalized stimulation, field estimation, and guidance of TMS/TES protocols.

topics.title: Neural Dynamics of Curiosity and Memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how curiosity, imagination, and episodic simulation shape neural representations during naturalistic experience and memory processes. It also includes work on long-term dynamics, reinstatement, and related biases such as misinformation and memory distortion.

keywords: the neural correlates of selective attention
the influence of visual perceptual load
topics.title: Neural correlates of selective attention and perception
topics.description: This cluster centers on how selective attention, perceptual load, expectation, and prior knowledge shape conscious detection and perception. The studies emphasize neural correlates and EEG-based dynamics across visual and auditory tasks, often within predictive coding and statistical learning frameworks.

keywords: functional connectivity correlates of observed in session rumination
topics.title: Rumination and Connectivity in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on how rumination and depressive symptoms relate to functional connectivity, network change, and predictive fMRI in major depressive disorder. It also includes work on lesion localization and clinical interventions such as CBT and pharmacotherapy.

topics.title: Neural and Language Markers of Dyslexia
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how language processing and brain network measures relate to dyslexia and reading disability, including predictive language mechanisms and neural entrainment. It also highlights imaging-based markers such as white matter atlases, thalamic signals, and resting-state variability for prediction and characterization.

keywords: decode voice identity in auditory working memory
topics.title: Neural mechanisms of working memory
topics.description: This cluster centers on how working memory is maintained, manipulated, and represented across sensory modalities, especially auditory and visual content. It also highlights neural mechanisms involving oscillations, prefrontal and parietal regions, and broader network contributions to flexible memory storage.

topics.title: Hippocampal Memory Representation and Consolidation
topics.description: This cluster centers on hippocampal involvement in encoding, retrieval, and consolidation of episodic and recognition memory. It also highlights functional divisions along the hippocampal long axis, connectivity, and neural activity patterns underlying memory representation.

keywords: bold asl base cerebrovascular reactivity mapping
large scale 15o]water total body pet investigation
topics.title: Cerebrovascular Reactivity and Perfusion Imaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on measuring cerebrovascular reactivity and baseline perfusion/blood flow using ASL, BOLD, and PET approaches. The abstracts emphasize resting-state and hypercapnia-based mapping, along with normative reference modeling across age and disease contexts.

topics.title: High-Resolution Ultra-High-Field fMRI Methods
topics.description: The cluster centers on ultra-high-field, high-resolution fMRI acquisition and reconstruction approaches, emphasizing denoising, protocol optimization, and submillimeter imaging. It also includes mapping and acceleration strategies for improved BOLD sensitivity and mesoscale functional measurements.

keywords: abnormal functional hierarchy development during childhood in autism
topics.title: Autism Development and Brain Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster focuses on autism and autism spectrum disorder across childhood and adolescence, emphasizing developmental changes in brain organization and connectivity. It highlights functional neuroimaging, EEG, and structural alterations associated with autistic individuals and youth.

topics.title: Neural Bases of Multilingual Language Processing
topics.description: This cluster centers on how language is represented and processed in the brain, especially in multilingual and bilingual speakers. It includes fMRI and neural encoding studies of speech, reading, lateralization, and cortical specialization.

keywords: adaptive math training drives network reorganization
development and evaluation of an intervention for adolescents
topics.title: Developmental Dyscalculia and Math Network Plasticity
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental dyscalculia, math performance, and arithmetic fact retrieval, with emphasis on how training or intervention relates to changes in numerical and working memory networks. The abstracts highlight altered network integration, effective connectivity, and longitudinal changes across children and adolescents.

topics.title: Cortical folding and neonatal brain atlases
topics.description: This cluster centers on developmental patterns of cortical folding, gyrification, and brain shape across fetal, neonatal, and infant stages. It also includes atlas construction and segmentation resources for mapping developing brains and supporting normative trajectory analysis.

keywords: genetic correlation of cortical folding patterns
topics.title: Normative Development of Cortical Structure
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cortical structure and folding change across human development and the lifespan, with emphasis on normative modeling and anatomical organization. It also links these patterns to genetic, epigenetic, and brain-body associations.

keywords: cortical microarchitecture along major vascular territories
topics.title: Multiscale Cortical Organization and Neurochemistry
topics.description: This cluster centers on how cortical microarchitecture, multimodal maps, and neurochemical patterns vary across brain regions and species. It emphasizes hierarchical organization, similarity modeling, and trade-offs that link structure, function, and receptor distributions.

topics.title: Parietal Coding of Space, Number, and Language
topics.description: This cluster centers on how the human parietal cortex organizes allocentric coding, topographic principles, and map-like representations for space, time, and numerical processing. It also links these representations to supramodal working memory, sensory simulation, imagined speech and language, with some studies using TMS.

keywords: cortical depth profiles of mental imagery
spatiotemporal dynamics of visual mental imagery of simple and complex content
topics.title: Visual Mental Imagery and Individual Variation
topics.description: This cluster centers on the neural basis of visual mental imagery, including differences across individuals and atypical populations such as aphantasics and congenitally blind individuals. It emphasizes fMRI-based evidence, cortical depth profiles, and spatiotemporal dynamics in the human visual system.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
fast accurate population receptive field prf mapping
topics.title: Neural Representations and Visual Specialization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how deep neural networks and fMRI are used to probe neural representations, representational hierarchy, and voxel selectivity in human visual cortex. It also includes work on mapping visual specialization, aligning model and brain representations, and relating these patterns to behavior and natural scenes.

topics.title: Naturalistic Language Processing and Brain Networks
topics.description: Studies examine how the brain supports natural speech, sentence, and narrative comprehension through predictive coding and hierarchical language processing. The cluster also highlights network organization, including auditory and language systems, spatial dissociation, and interactions with multiple-demand regions.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Connectome and Symptom Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster centers on shared transdiagnostic patterns of structural and functional brain connectivity across psychiatric disorders, including bipolar disorder, depression, and early psychosis. It also emphasizes multimodal biomarkers, symptom dimensions, and genetic underpinnings linked to clinical phenotypes and functional outcome.

topics.title: Adolescent Neurodevelopment and Mental Health Risk
topics.description: This cluster focuses on the ABCD study and related work on neurodevelopment during childhood and adolescence. It emphasizes how adversity, environmental exposure, and risk relate to mental health outcomes such as ADHD and anxiety/depression.

topics.title: Suicide Risk in Mood Disorders
topics.description: This cluster focuses on suicidal ideation, attempts, and broader suicide risk across major depressive disorder, bipolar disorder, and adolescent depression. It also highlights brain network dysfunction and connectivity alterations as possible neurobiological correlates of suicidality.

topics.title: Reliability of Resting-State fMRI Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on evaluating the reliability, stability, and reproducibility of resting-state fMRI measures, especially functional connectivity estimates and network-level signals. It also emphasizes methodological factors such as motion correction strategies, scan length, and precision fMRI that affect measurement quality.

keywords: individualized parcellation reveal dorsal attention network specificity
multi scale dynamics of cognitive network engagement
transient attentional events shape functional lateralization
topics.title: Naturalistic Attention and Dynamic Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how attention and cognitive network engagement are represented in naturalistic settings, especially during movie viewing and fMRI. It emphasizes individual differences, dynamic neural trajectories, and individualized parcellation approaches to reveal attention-network specificity and functional lateralization.

topics.title: Brain Dynamics and Consciousness Integration
topics.description: This cluster centers on how large-scale brain dynamics, network organization, and information flow relate to brain states and consciousness. It also includes work on nonlinear dynamics, synchronization, and multilevel integration in resting and task conditions.

topics.title: Structural Connectivity and Human Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how structural connectome organization supports human brain communication, including long- and short-range connections, hemispheric asymmetry, and subcortical roles. It also emphasizes tractography, network analysis, and microstructural bases of structure-function coupling in resting-state networks.

topics.title: Genetic and brain correlates of schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster centers on schizophrenia and related psychosis-spectrum symptoms, emphasizing polygenic risk, genomic associations, and genetic-neural pathways. It also links these factors to structural brain variation, cortical and hippocampal features, and functional network connectivity across youth and the general population.

topics.title: Dynamic Brain Connectivity and States
topics.description: This cluster focuses on modeling how brain connectivity and brain states change over time, especially in fMRI data. It emphasizes dynamic functional and effective connectivity, forecasting state transitions, and links to arousal and cognitive function.

keywords: personalized fc- e field guide precision tms fmri
topics.title: Noninvasive Brain Stimulation and Connectivity
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive brain stimulation approaches and their effects on brain function, connectivity, and hemodynamics across imaging-guided protocols. It includes TMS, ultrasound, and related multimodal stimulation studies aimed at network-level modulation and personalized targeting.

topics.title: Multiscale brain dynamics across the lifespan
topics.description: This cluster centers on how brain dynamics and structure-function relationships vary across different resolutions and across the adult lifespan. It emphasizes multiscale neurophysiological measures, variability, and hierarchical organization in understanding functional integration and segregation.

keywords: multimodal neuroimaging signatures of data driven symptom phenotypes
topics.title: Multimodal Signatures of Schizophrenia Subtypes
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying data-driven schizophrenia and psychosis subtypes using multimodal neuroimaging and related brain features. It emphasizes heterogeneity, neurofunctional and structural signatures, and links to symptom dimensions and transdiagnostic features.

keywords: altered local traveling wave propagation directions
cortical excitability mapping reveal schizophrenia subtypes with distinct molecular signatures
treatment resistant non treatment resistant schizophrenia
topics.title: Dynamic Brain Network Alterations in Schizophrenia
topics.description: This cluster focuses on schizophrenia-related disruptions in large-scale brain dynamics, connectivity, and microstate organization, often linking these patterns to symptom subtypes and treatment response. It also highlights candidate biomarkers and subtype-specific molecular signatures across imaging and electrophysiological measures.

keywords: bayesian non parametric serial mri harmonization
a tool for transparent and standardized assessment of multisite harmonization in neuroimaging
topics.title: Multisite Neuroimaging Harmonization Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on methods and tools for harmonizing multisite neuroimaging and related data, with attention to batch effects, site heterogeneity, and standardized evaluation. It includes statistical frameworks and open-source approaches for assessing and improving harmonization efficacy across studies.

keywords: neuromark functional networks be robust to spatial normalization template
spectral modes of brain functional connectivity
topics.title: Reproducible Functional Connectivity and Network Variability
topics.description: This cluster centers on reproducibility, effect size, and sample-size considerations in fMRI and resting-state functional connectivity studies. It also emphasizes spatial normalization, personalized networks, and variability in brain functional organization across analyses and templates.

topics.title: Structure-Function Connectivity in Brain Networks
topics.description: This cluster centers on how structural and functional brain organization relate across connectomes, parcellations, and multimodal network analyses. It also emphasizes mapping and reliability of connectivity profiles, including directed and effective communication measures.

keywords: fmri base brain function symptom integration
topics.title: Depression Subtypes and Brain Biomarkers
topics.description: This cluster focuses on identifying clinically distinct depression subtypes and linking them to brain connectivity, symptom profiles, and biomarker patterns. It also emphasizes multimodal prediction and associations with neuroimmune, metabolic, and cardiometabolic risk factors.

topics.title: Human Cortical Atlases and Comparative Mapping
topics.description: This cluster focuses on constructing, comparing, and refining human and nonhuman primate brain atlases for cortical organization, cytoarchitecture, and anatomical correspondence. It also includes work on common coordinate frameworks, surface-based registration, and segmentation methods that support cross-species comparison.

keywords: deep learning for automated segmentation of medial temporal lobe subregions
topics.title: Deep Learning for Brain MRI Segmentation and Reconstruction
topics.description: This cluster centers on deep learning methods for brain MRI, especially reconstruction, automated segmentation, and parcellation tasks. It also includes domain adaptation and synthetic training strategies to improve robustness and data efficiency across neuroimaging applications.

topics.title: Network Alterations in Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression-related alterations in brain networks, including functional topology, effective connectivity, and region-specific hyperconnectivity. It spans major depressive disorder subtypes and related mood-anxiety conditions, with emphasis on personalized network organization and large-scale functional changes.

keywords: brain state dynamics during emotional reflection
topics.title: Dynamic brain states in depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on depression and major depressive disorder, emphasizing dynamic brain state organization during emotional reflection and resting-state network coupling. It also links depressive vulnerability to peripheral inflammation, metabolic modulation, and neurobiological markers.

keywords: longitudinal baboon brain fetal mri database
topics.title: Mesoscale MRI and Brain Microstructure Mapping
topics.description: These studies center on high-resolution MRI and related imaging approaches for mapping brain microstructure, cortical lamination, and mesoscale connectivity across development and ex vivo preparations. The cluster includes human and baboon fetal brain studies, organoid models, and advanced histological or diffusion-based methods.

keywords: the analytic flexibility of processing pipelines
topics.title: White Matter Morphometry and Tractography
topics.description: This cluster centers on white matter structure, tractography-derived connectivity, and morphometric analysis across brain bundles and the corpus callosum. It also emphasizes methodological sensitivity and analytic flexibility in pipelines and tractometry.

keywords: multimodal brain connectivity integration for cognitive performance prediction in youth
topics.title: Individualized Connectome-Based Brain-Behavior Prediction
topics.description: This cluster centers on predicting cognitive and behavioral differences from individualized brain connectivity and functional network features. It emphasizes connectome-based modeling, personalized parcellations, and multimodal integration for mapping brain-behavior relationships.

topics.title: Individual Connectome Fingerprinting in Neuroimaging
topics.description: This cluster centers on identifying stable, individual-specific brain and connectome fingerprints across fMRI and other neuroimaging contexts. It also includes methods for improving fingerprint generalization, replication, and feature selection in connectome analysis.

keywords: manifold prediction for white matter bundle parametrization
topics.title: White Matter Tractography and Bundle Analysis
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI tractography methods for segmenting, parametrizing, and analyzing white matter bundles. It also includes approaches for detecting white matter abnormalities and lesions across individuals and sites.

keywords: large multi center study diffusion mri protocols
topics.title: Multisite Diffusion MRI Reliability and Microstructure
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI methods for probing white matter microstructure, with emphasis on diffusional kurtosis and related signal models. It also highlights multisite evaluation, scanner effects, and reproducibility across protocols and test-retest settings.

keywords: graph metrics predict electroconvulsive therapy response
subcallosal cingulate functional connectivity link to remission after electroconvulsive therapy
topics.title: Brain Connectivity Predicts Depression Treatment Response
topics.description: This cluster focuses on depression and related affective disorders, especially how brain connectivity and network metrics relate to treatment response. A major emphasis is on electroconvulsive therapy and remission prediction using functional connectivity, controllability, and graph-based brain network measures.

keywords: efficacy of personalized connectivity guide brain stimulation in treatment resistant depression
topics.title: Personalized Brain Stimulation for Depression
topics.description: This cluster centers on personalized neuromodulation approaches for treatment resistant and major depressive disorder, especially connectivity-guided targeting such as left DLPFC stimulation and Stanford Neuromodulation Therapy. It also includes neurophysiological signatures, frontoparietal connectivity changes, and treatment efficacy outcomes.
